title: GatewayClass vs Gateway
description:
## GatewayClass vs Gateway

Gateway API defines separate `GatewayClass` and `Gateway` resources.

What is the relationship between a `GatewayClass` and a `Gateway`?

options.text: A GatewayClass acts as a namespace-scoped backup for a cluster-wide Gateway
A Gateway references a GatewayClass via the spec.gatewayClassName field to inherit its controller configuration and behaviour
A GatewayClass is generated automatically for each new Gateway resource created in the cluster
Gateways and GatewayClasses are synonyms and can be used interchangeably

explanation: Much like a `Pod` references a `RuntimeClass` or a `PersistentVolumeClaim` references a `StorageClass`, a `Gateway` references a `GatewayClass` via `spec.gatewayClassName`. The GatewayClass specifies the `controllerName` that should handle and provision the network infrastructure for that Gateway instance.

hints.title: Analogy to StorageClass
hints.body: Just as a StorageClass defines the provisioner and parameter template for a PV, a GatewayClass defines the controller implementation for a Gateway.
